Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student enrolled in the TechXera Campus, containing personal and academic information necessary for their profile and dashboard.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

studentId.description: The unique identification number or student ID used by the student for login purposes. (This is distinct from the internal 'id' PK).

firstName.description: The first name of the student.

lastName.description: The last name of the student.

email.description: The email address of the student.

enrollmentDate.description: The date and time when the student was officially enrolled.

currentSemester.description: The current academic semester the student is enrolled in (e.g., 'Fall 2023', 'Semester 1').

Result.title: Result
Result.description: Stores academic results for a specific student, including marks, grades, and associated details for a subject or examination.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Result entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity this result belongs to. (Relationship: Student 1:N Result)

subject.description: The academic subject for which this result is recorded.

semester.description: The academic semester in which this result was obtained.

marks.description: The numerical marks achieved by the student in the subject.

grade.description: The letter grade or equivalent awarded for the subject (e.g., 'A+', 'B', 'Pass').

examDate.description: The date and time when the examination or assessment was conducted.

performanceNotes.description: Optional administrative notes or comments regarding the student's performance.

StudyMaterial.title: StudyMaterial
StudyMaterial.description: Represents educational resources such as previous questions, notes, or other study aids that students can access and download.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudyMaterial entity.

title.description: The title or name of the study material.

description.description: A brief description of the study material's content or purpose.

fileUrl.description: The URL or URI pointing to the downloadable file for the study material.

subject.description: The academic subject category to which the study material belongs.

semester.description: The academic semester for which the study material is primarily relevant.

materialType.description: The type of the study material (e.g., 'Previous Question', 'Notes', 'Book', 'Video Lecture').

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the study material was uploaded to the system.

Notice.title: Notice
Notice.description: Represents an important announcement or notice displayed on the dynamic notice board for students.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notice entity.

title.description: The concise title of the notice.

description.description: The full content or detailed message of the notice.

publishDate.description: The date and time when the notice was officially published.

isUrgent.description: A flag indicating whether the notice is urgent and requires visual highlighting.

expiryDate.description: The date and time after which the notice is no longer considered active or should be removed.

definition.description: Stores individual student profiles. The document ID (`{studentUid}`) is the Firebase Authentication User ID. Includes fields like `studentId` (internal ID), `firstName`, `lastName`, etc. Student can read/update their own profile. Admin can read/update/delete any student profile.

params.name: studentUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the student, serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores academic results specific to a student. Each document includes a denormalized `studentId` field that **MUST** match the parent `{studentUid}` for authorization independence. Students can read their own results. Admin can read/update/delete any student's results.

params.name: studentUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the student owner of these results.

params.name: resultId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific academic result document.

definition.description: Houses general study resources accessible by all authenticated students for reading. Admin users have full CRUD permissions. No specific user ownership is tied to individual documents for read access.

params.name: materialId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific study material document.

definition.description: Contains important announcements for all authenticated students to read. Admin users have full CRUD permissions. No specific user ownership is tied to individual documents for read access.

params.name: noticeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific notice document.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document at this path for a given `request.auth.uid` grants administrative privileges across the application. Documents typically contain minimal data, serving as a marker. The schema '$ref' points to 'Student' as a placeholder for admin identity, but a dedicated 'Admin' entity could be created if more specific admin profile data is needed.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable by strictly adhering to the mandated design principles. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
1.  **Student-Owned Data (`/students/{studentUid}/results/{resultId}`):** Each 'Result' document explicitly contains a `studentId` field. This field is denormalized and **MUST** match the `{studentUid}` wildcard in the path, which in turn maps to `request.auth.uid`. This direct mapping eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in security rules to check ownership. Rules can simply verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.studentId` or `request.auth.uid == studentUid` (from the path). This enables atomic write operations for results without parent document lookups.
2.  **Global Data (`/studyMaterials/{materialId}`, `/notices/{noticeId}`):** These collections are accessed globally by all authenticated users for read operations. Admin access for CRUD is determined by the existence of a document in the `/roles_admin/{adminUid}` collection. Since access is not dependent on specific parent document attributes, no denormalization is required beyond the inherent document data.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Student-Specific Data (`/students/{studentUid}`, `/students/{studentUid}/results/{resultId}`):**
    *   For a student to list their own data, queries will include `where('studentId', '==', request.auth.uid)` or naturally be scoped by the path, e.g., `db.collection('students').doc(request.auth.uid).collection('results').get()`. The security rules will enforce that `request.auth.uid == studentUid` (from the path) for any read access, ensuring only the owner can list their own items. This is a query that the rules can evaluate and allow, not filter.
2.  **Global Read-Only Data (`/studyMaterials/{materialId}`, `/notices/{noticeId}`):**
    *   Authenticated users can list all documents in these collections (`db.collection('studyMaterials').get()`). The security rules will simply check `request.auth.uid != null` for read operations. This naturally supports `list` operations without requiring client-side filtering or complex server-side rule logic, fulfilling the QAP principle.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
*   **Private Student Profiles:** `/students/{studentUid}` uses path-based ownership, making student profile data directly linked to their Firebase Auth UID. Only the owning student or an admin can access it.
*   **Nested User-Owned Data:** `/students/{studentUid}/results/{resultId}` continues the hierarchical path, ensuring that a student's results are securely nested under their profile. This leverages Firebase's strong security model for user-owned data.
*   **Public/Collaborative Data (for Authenticated Users):** `/studyMaterials` and `/notices` are distinct top-level collections because they have a homogeneous security posture: read-only for all authenticated students, and full CRUD for administrators. This prevents mixing security contexts within a single collection.
*   **DBAC (Database Access Control):** A dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{adminUid}` is used for admin role management. The existence of a document at `request.auth.uid` in this collection grants administrative privileges, embodying the 'existence over content' strategy and completely avoiding custom claims.
